bugs.error_message: App not working after antigravity edits; Quick Match button does nothing. Console: Refused to execute script .../_next/static/... MIME text/html, 404 on all chunks
bugs.root_cause: apps/web/.next deleted while next-server still running; server served 404/HTML for every static chunk so React never hydrated and all buttons were dead. Source edits were fine (typecheck clean).
bugs.fix: Killed stale next-server, restarted pnpm dev (web) to rebuild .next. Verified Quick Match creates computer game vs Stockfish (Level 4), engine played 1.Nf3.

bugs.root_cause: Property access on potentially null/undefined value
bugs.fix: Added null safety (optional chaining or null check)

bugs.error_message: POST /api/computer-games/:id/move -> 500 Internal server error. Prisma: Unique constraint failed on the fields: (gameId, ply) at tx.move.create (computer-games.service.ts:252)
bugs.root_cause: When the side-to-moves clock has flagged, engine applyMove() (game-state.ts isTimeout early-return) returns the game as completed WITHOUT appending the attempted move. submitMove ignored this: it took state.moves[last] (the PREVIOUS move) and re-inserted it, duplicating its ply -> unique (gameId,ply) violation -> 500. Triggered whenever a computer game sat idle past its time control.
bugs.fix: In submitMove, after applyMove, guard: if state.moves.length === engineState.moves.length (no move appended = flag fall), persist game as completed with state.result/resultReason (timeout) and return WITHOUT creating a Move row. Verified: flagged game now returns 200 status=completed result=white_wins resultReason=timeout, no dup row; normal moves ply1-3 still 200.

bugs.root_cause: lg: min-h-0 lg:flex-1"> → h-full lg:min-h-0 lg:justify-center',; sm: p-3 lg:h-full lg:w-auto lg:max-h-full"> → p-3">
bugs.fix: Changed lg: min-h-0 lg:flex-1"> → h-full lg:min-h-0 lg:justify-center',; sm: p-3 lg:h-full lg:w-auto lg:max-h-full"> → p-3">

bugs.root_cause: No early return/throw for edge case: !theme || storeAppTheme === theme
bugs.fix: Added guard clause: if (!theme || storeAppTheme === theme)

bugs.error_message: Uncaught Error: Maximum update depth exceeded. ... at ThemeSync (src/app/providers.tsx)
bugs.root_cause: PRE-EXISTING. ThemeSync had two bidirectional effects (theme->store via update(); store->theme via setTheme()). When the persisted settings-store appTheme disagreed with next-themes theme on load (e.g. localStorage purechess-settings appTheme='dark' but next-themes theme='system'), the two effects ping-ponged forever and never converged -> React 'Maximum update depth exceeded'. ThemeSync is global in Providers so it crashed EVERY page including the untouched homepage. Browser-localStorage triggered (not caused by the game redesign); surfaces for any user whose stored appTheme differs from next-themes. Restarting the dev server did NOT fix it; clearing localStorage did (confirming the trigger).
bugs.fix: Replaced the two effects with ONE reconciliation effect + a lastSynced ref. Persisted store is source of truth (store->setTheme); next-themes changes mirror back to the store only when they did NOT originate from our own sync (ref guard breaks the echo). Verified live via chrome-devtools: appTheme='dark' + theme='system' loads with html data-theme=dark, store/next-themes both converge to dark, zero console errors, no loop. SettingsForm (sets both store+theme) and the (demo)/demo page (sets theme only) both still work.

bugs.error_message: TypeError: activeRef.current?.scrollIntoView is not a function (move-panel.tsx) in jsdom review-page tests
bugs.root_cause: New MovePanel called activeRef.current?.scrollIntoView({...}); jsdom elements do not implement scrollIntoView, so the method is undefined and invoking it throws. The original review-move-list guarded with an optional CALL.
bugs.fix: Use optional call: activeRef.current?.scrollIntoView?.({ block: 'nearest' }). 7 review-page tests went green.

bugs.error_message: Playing vs Stockfish, idle a few mins, return shows game already lost but no checkmate
bugs.root_cause: Computer games were created with a finite server clock (timeControlSeconds from the setup time-control picker). Engine applyMove -> isTimeout flags on real wall-clock elapsed since last move (nowMs - lastTickAt), so idling between moves drained the human clock and the next submit returned status=completed reason=timeout. But the in-game UI hardcodes clock "∞" / Time "Unlimited" and shows no countdown, so the timeout loss was an unexplained surprise (no checkmate).
bugs.fix: Made vs-computer games untimed to match the UI. submitMove now resets engineState.clock.lastTickAt = nowMs before applyMove, so isTimeout sees ~0 elapsed and never fires regardless of idle time. Removed the time-control picker from computer-game-setup.tsx; it now sends fixed UNTIMED_SECONDS=600/inc=0 only to satisfy the DTO/category column. Kept the existing flag-fall guard as a defensive dead path (bug-005 mock test still passes). typecheck clean (api+web), 16/16 service spec green.

bugs.error_message: Playing vs Stockfish, idle a few mins, return shows game already lost but no checkmate
bugs.root_cause: Computer games were created with a finite server clock (timeControlSeconds from the setup time-control picker). But the in-game UI hardcodes clock "∞" / Time "Unlimited" and shows no countdown, so the timeout loss was an unexplained surprise (no checkmate).
bugs.fix: Made vs-computer games untimed to match the UI. submitMove now resets engineState.clock.lastTickAt = nowMs before applyMove, so isTimeout sees ~0 elapsed and never fires regardless of idle time. Removed the time-control picker from computer-game-setup.tsx; it now sends fixed UNTIMED_SECONDS=600/inc=0 only to satisfy the DTO/category column. Kept the existing flag-fall guard as a defensive dead path (bug-005 mock test still passes). typecheck clean (api+web), 16/16 service spec green.

bugs.error_message: session 09 (a11y-polish) FAILED in 10m44s (exit 97): declared deliverables missing from session diff: apps/web/test/computer-game/use-game-keyboard.test.ts (not in session diff)
bugs.root_cause: Two layers. (1) Plan declared the keyboard-hook test at apps/web/test/computer-game/use-game-keyboard.test.ts but the hook lives at src/hooks/, so run 1's agent wrote the test to test/hooks/. (2) Deeper cause: the sonnet session is NON-DETERMINISTIC about test file paths/names across reruns. Run 1 produced test/hooks/use-game-keyboard.test.ts + test/computer-game/live-announcer.test.tsx; run 2 (after fixing the declared path) produced a single test/computer-game/a11y.test.tsx instead. The epic deliverables gate matches declared produces paths exactly, so chasing exact filenames was whack-a-mole. The two src deliverables (hook + live-announcer.tsx) were stable across runs; only the test path drifted.
bugs.fix: Changed the test deliverable in session-09 plan touches+produces from an exact path to a glob: apps/web/test/*.test.ts* . The validator compiles globs to regex where * becomes .* and crosses / (validate-session-deliverables.py _matches_declared), so it matches any test under apps/web/test/ regardless of subdir/name. Kept the two stable src files exact. Each resume also requires pruning the stale epic/vs-computer--s09 worktree+branch (always ahead of trunk after a failed run, so the runner refuses to delete it and aborts at exit 1). Verified run 2's actual diff (test/computer-game/a11y.test.tsx) matches the glob.

bugs.error_message: font-display tailwind class rendered as Geist fallback — Fraunces never applied on hero headline
bugs.root_cause: tailwind.config.ts fontFamily.display added while next dev was running; tailwind does not regenerate utilities on config change without a dev-server restart, so .font-display had no CSS rule
bugs.fix: added .font-display { font-family: var(--font-display), Georgia, serif } to globals.css @layer utilities (hot-reloads); tailwind key kept for builds

bugs.root_cause: No early return/throw for edge case: reasonLabel === 'Checkmate'

bugs.error_message: Cannot find module '.../argon2@0.31.2/.../lib/binding/napi-v3/argon2.node' MODULE_NOT_FOUND at API startup (dist/main.js)
bugs.root_cause: argon2 native prebuilt binary missing — install script (node-pre-gyp) never ran/completed for this pnpm store entry. pnpm rebuild argon2 was a silent no-op.
bugs.fix: cd node_modules/.pnpm/argon2@0.31.2/node_modules/argon2 && npx node-pre-gyp install --fallback-to-build — downloads prebuilt darwin-arm64 napi-v3 binary from GitHub releases. Verified hash works under Node 24.
Changed to "docker compose up -d postgres redis"

bugs.error_message: pnpm infra:up fails: napi build in apps/api/Dockerfile — error: linker 'aarch64-linux-musl-gcc' not found (Rust engine cross-compile on Apple Silicon)
bugs.root_cause: infra:up ran 'docker compose up -d' which builds ALL services incl. api/web images. api Dockerfile's Rust napi step assumes x86_64 Alpine; on arm64 host the Alpine image is aarch64 and cargo wants aarch64-linux-musl-gcc, not installed. Local dev only needs postgres+redis.
bugs.fix: Changed infra:up to 'docker compose up -d postgres redis'; added infra:up:full for all services. arm64 Docker image build of api still broken — needs aarch64 musl toolchain or RUSTFLAGS linker=cc in Dockerfile if ever required.

bugs.error_message: Untimed vs-computer game ended 'You lost BY TIMEOUT' after ~10 min idle
bugs.root_cause: Setup sent dummy timeControlSeconds=600 for 'Untimed' while the server's untimed convention is <= 0 (and even 0 would flag instantly because isTimeout uses remaining<=elapsed with a 0ms clock). The earlier unconditional lastTickAt reset was made conditional on <=0 when the time picker was re-added, so 'untimed' games ran a real 10-minute clock with no visible countdown.
bugs.fix: Client sends timeControlSeconds=0 for untimed. Server: engineTimeMs() gives untimed games a ~100h sentinel clock (UNTIMED_CLOCK_MS=359999000) in both create paths + truncateToPly; getCategory(<=0)=rapid; computeExtras infers untimed from both sides >= sentinel and returns clock:null so clients render no countdown.

bugs.error_message: Mobile (<lg) computer-game page: right rail overlapped the board bottom + 'You' player strip
bugs.root_cause: GameShell board cell had unprefixed min-h-0; in the stacked mobile grid (definite h-full shorter than content) the cell was the only row allowed to shrink below content, compressing 608px of board column into 461px so the rail rendered on top of it.
bugs.fix: Cell class changed to lg:min-h-0 — shrinkable only in the desktop height-bounded layout; mobile rows size to content and the grid scrolls.
Changed transform: scale(1) → translateY(-52px) scale(1); opacity: 1 → 0

bugs.root_cause: No early return/throw for edge case: !_isPlaying || !liveClock || state.game.status !== 'active'

bugs.error_message: Board theme picker (Mono) silently no-oped — board stayed classic green/cream
bugs.root_cause: [data-board-theme='mono'] (specificity 0,1,0) lost to the dark-mode re-pin `:root:not([data-theme='light'])` (0,2,0) which redeclares the classic --board-sq-* values.
bugs.fix: Selector raised to :root[data-board-theme='mono'] (0,2,0, later in source).

bugs.error_message: /games crashed client-side: Cannot read properties of null (reading 'username'); then redirected to a 404 because /login did not exist
bugs.root_cause: GET /api/auth/me returns 200 {user:null} when unauthenticated (never 4xx), so `if (!result)` passed and result.user.username threw. Separately the app had NO /login or /register routes — home's 'Create account' and every auth redirect 404ed.
bugs.fix: Guard `!result || !result.user`; built /login + /register pages (AuthShell + forms against POST /api/auth/login|register, return-param aware). UserMenu now self-fetches /api/auth/me via TanStack Query (was hardcoded user={null}) and Sign out calls POST /api/auth/logout (was a dead /logout link).

bugs.error_message: Review page for computer games showed 'Dec 31, 1969', rating '0' for the human and bare '4' as the computer's rating
bugs.root_cause: buildReviewFromComputerGame hardcoded startedAt=new Date(0) (epoch) and stuffed computerLevel into rating; review-client rendered both raw.
bugs.fix: startedAt='' (client omits the Date row), client renders 'Level N' for id==='computer', hides rating 0, adds Bot/User avatars; Time label now Timed/Untimed from clock presence.

bugs.error_message: Invite creator stuck on 'Waiting for opponent' forever — acceptance never navigated to the game; accepted invites then dead-ended because /play/:gameId did not exist
bugs.root_cause: useInviteSocket opened a RAW WebSocket against the socket.io InviteGateway (protocol mismatch — handshake never completes) AND the gateway emitted to user:{id} rooms that no connection ever joined. Even if it had worked, the push target /play/:gameId was an unrouted path and the games API was a stub.
bugs.fix: Built the full live PvP surface: GamesService/Controller (state/move/resign, engine-validated, idle flag-fall on poll), PvpGameStateDto, /play/[gameId] polling live page; invite-create now polls GET /invites/:token every 2s and navigates when status='active'.
